title: Buildx Builders, Multi-Platform Images, Bake, and OCI Outputs · Delivery Case: Docker Build Platform Arguments
summary: A CLI release must support linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 without silently shipping an emulated, untested, or single-platform image. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker build platform arguments increment in multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use BUILDPLATFORM, TARGETPLATFORM, TARGETOS, TARGETARCH, and stage pinning without accidentally executing target binaries on the builder.
Choose emulation, multiple native nodes, or language cross-compilation and verify build and target platform separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Build Platform Arguments · Theory Docker Build Platform Arguments
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Use BUILDPLATFORM, TARGETPLATFORM, TARGETOS, TARGETARCH, and stage pinning without accidentally executing target binaries on the builder.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Build Platform Arguments contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Build Platform Arguments · Theory Docker Build Platform Arguments.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use buildplatform, targetplatform, targetos, targetarch, and stage pinning without accidentally executing target binaries on the builder. For Predict Docker Build Platform Arguments · Theory Docker Build Platform Arguments, keep Docker Build Platform Arguments connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Use BUILDPLATFORM, TARGETPLATFORM, TARGETOS, TARGETARCH, and stage pinning without accidentally executing target binaries on the builder. A defensible solution applies Docker Build Platform Arguments, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-multiplatform-buildx-bake-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-multiplatform-buildx-bake-5
    artifact: docker-bake.hcl
    decision: worked docker multiplatform buildx bake 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      target "release" {
      platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
      attest = ["type=provenance", "type=sbom"]
      output = ["type=registry"]
      }
    verify: docker buildx bake --print release && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate
    failure: Reject architecture emulation as native test evidence, local image load for a multi-platform index, or platform-dependent inputs without declarations.
    repair: Inspect builder capabilities, make target platform explicit, test each artifact, publish the index, and verify per-platform digest selection.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Predefined platform arguments are automatically inherited inside every stage. This fails the Docker Build Platform Arguments contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use BUILDPLATFORM, TARGETPLATFORM, TARGETOS, TARGETARCH, and stage pinning without accidentally executing target binaries on the builder.
